text: The surgeon uses the Enseal tissue sealer to perform blunt and sharp dissection at the hilum, separating the upper lobe branch of the superior pulmonary vein from its adventitia and the pulmonary artery. After isolating the vein, it is divided using a stapler.

The surgeon uses the Enseal device to separate the adventitia from the pulmonary artery, isolating the truncus anterior, which is then divided using the Echelon stapler. The curved jaws of the instrument facilitate the dissection of lymph nodes at station ten from the mediastinum.

The surgeon isolates the bronchus and removes the station eleven lymph node to facilitate bronchus isolation. A bleed from a bronchial artery is efficiently controlled using the Enseal device. Lymphatic tissue is removed from around the bronchus, and the recurrent branch of the pulmonary artery is divided without additional ligature clips.

The surgeon divides the bronchus and dissects the mediastinal pleura, separating the right upper lobe bronchus from the posterior chest wall. The Enseal device is used to safely dissect the posterior mediastinal pleura. The horizontal fissure is divided, followed by the division of the oblique fissure, completing these steps in a safe manner.
